description: All secrets must be stored in environment variables or secret managers, never in code or config files
example: Use .env files locally, vault/KMS in production

description: All user-facing endpoints must require authentication unless explicitly documented as public
example: JWT, OAuth 2.0, or session-based auth required

description: PII and sensitive data must be encrypted at rest and in transit
example: TLS 1.2+ for transit, AES-256 for rest

description: All dependencies must be scanned for known vulnerabilities before deployment
All production code changes must be reviewed by at least one other developer or AI agent
All new features must include unit tests; critical paths must have integration tests
Code must pass configured linters with zero errors before commit
Follow language-specific naming conventions consistently across the codebase
example: camelCase for JS/TS, snake_case for Python

description: All public APIs must have documented endpoints, parameters, and response formats
example: OpenAPI/Swagger for REST, GraphQL schema for GraphQL

description: Significant architecture decisions must be documented with rationale
All user-facing changes must be documented in a changelog
Development, staging, and production environments must be as similar as possible
Every deployment must have a documented rollback procedure
example: Database migration rollbacks, blue-green or canary

description: Production systems must have health checks, logging, and alerting configured
